items.front: Who determines which runway or runways are designated runway in use, active runway, or duty runway?
items.back: The ATCT supervisor or controller in charge.

items.front: What runway is normally assigned when wind is 5 knots or more?
items.back: The runway most nearly aligned with the wind.

items.front: What runway is normally assigned when wind is less than 5 knots?
What two listed exceptions may override the normal wind-based runway selection rule?
items.back: Use of another runway is operationally advantageous, or a Runway Use Program is in effect.

items.front: What takes precedence over delay, capacity, and noise-abatement considerations in runway selection?
How may ATC handle a pilot request for a different runway?
items.back: ATC may honor it as soon as operationally practicable.

items.front: What must ATC tell a pilot if the requested runway is noise-sensitive?
items.back: Advise the pilot that the requested runway is noise-sensitive.

items.front: When may a designated STOL runway be assigned?
items.back: Only when requested by the pilot or as specified in a letter of agreement with an aircraft operator.

items.front: Is controller convenience by itself enough to assign a designated STOL runway?
What must ATC issue if the pilot requests the measured STOL runway length?
What letter-of-agreement relationship can authorize assignment of a designated STOL runway?
items.back: A letter of agreement with an aircraft operator.

items.front: Does asking for measured STOL runway length automatically authorize assignment of the STOL runway?
When a tailwind component exists, what wind information must always be stated?
Does ATIS replace the requirement to state wind direction and velocity when a tailwind exists?
What note is given about describing the wind in 3-5-3?
items.back: The wind may be described as calm when appropriate.

items.front: If a tailwind component exists, may ATC state only the tailwind component instead of wind direction and velocity?
